title: rhodanthemum atlanticum subsp. gelidum
intro: Rhodanthemum atlanticum subsp. gelidum, also known as the Azores white-aster, is a flowering plant species endemic to the Azores Islands, characterized by its bright white flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Rhodanthemum atlanticum subsp. gelidum include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential and to establish standardized dosages.
